tier: A4 / A-d: multi-volume APFS containers (one container, multiple APFS volumes sharing the space manager)
host: Host pre-check: apfsprogs apfsck v0.2.1 (-cw: report-crash + report-weird; -u/report-unknown is intentionally omitted because apfsck does not implement Apple's DEFRAG incompatible feature that newfs_apfs/S.A.K. set). The S.A.K. APFS writer CLI (sak_apfs_writer_cli.exe) formatted the multi-volume container. macOS-kernel mount + Apple native fsck_apfs is the certifying confirmation.
change: Implemented A-d multi-volume container format: the format request takes additional_volume_names; each extra volume gets its own object map (+ its tree), extent-ref tree, snap-meta tree, empty root file-system tree, and volume superblock, placed in a six-block set at the end of the first volume's reserved prefix, all sharing the one container space manager. The container superblock's nx_fs_oid array carries every volume's virtual superblock OID, the container object map maps each to its physical superblock, nx_next_oid clears all assigned virtual OIDs (superblock + root tree per volume), and each volume superblock stamps its own apfs_fs_index, virtual superblock/root-tree OIDs, and UUID. fsck_apfs requires nx_max_file_systems = ceil(container_bytes / 512 MiB) exactly, so the container is sized large enough that the formula covers every volume (the field is never overridden); the additional volumes' metadata must fit chunk 0, so the metadata-overflow tier is fail-closed for multi-volume.
scenario: sak_apfs_writer_cli format-image --size-bytes 1073741824 (1 GiB -> nx_max_file_systems 2) --volume-name SAKVOLA --additional-volume-name SAKVOLB produces a bare (no-GPT) APFS container holding two volumes. 64 MiB (nx_max_file_systems 1) two-volume formats are fail-closed with a clear nx_max_file_systems message.

evidence_apfsck.command: apfsck -cw <image> (apfsprogs v0.2.1, super.c patched to print per-volume ALLOCDBG fs_alloc_count vs walked block count)
evidence_apfsck.result: exit 0, clean. TWO 'ALLOCDBG fs_alloc_count=5 walked=5' lines (one per volume) prove apfsck parsed + cross-checked BOTH volume superblocks against their walked allocation. Negative control: corrupting the second volume's superblock (block 0xe3) makes apfsck report 'Object header: bad checksum in block 0xe3' and exit 1, proving the clean pass is meaningful.
evidence_apfsck.container_superblock: nx_max_file_systems=2, nx_fs_oid[0]=1026 nx_fs_oid[1]=1030 nx_fs_oid[2]=0, nx_next_oid=1032; vol0 superblock OID 1026 / root-tree OID 1028 / fs_index 0, vol1 superblock OID 1030 / root-tree OID 1031 / fs_index 1; distinct per-volume UUIDs.

evidence_unit: tests/unit/test_partition_manager_core.cpp: apfsWriter_formatsMultiVolumeContainer formats a 1 GiB two-volume container and asserts the container-superblock nx_max_file_systems/nx_fs_oid array/nx_next_oid, both volume superblocks' checksums + fs_index (0,1) + superblock OIDs (1026,1030) + root-tree OIDs (1028,1031) + names (SAKVOLA,SAKVOLB) + distinct UUIDs, that the reader still walks the first volume, and that a 64 MiB two-volume format is fail-closed on nx_max_file_systems. Full local core suite green; clang-format / lizard (CCN<=10, params<=5) / cppcheck (exhaustive) all clean.
kernel_mount: CERTIFIED 2026-06-26 in a macOS Sequoia VM (qemu/KVM recovery, apfs_kext 2332.101.1). The 1 GiB two-volume image (sha256 2B190481BEB04539BC052B3E87CA7FB432A698246AD8AABDDF07BA548A10F239) was attached on SATA (bare APFS container = /dev/disk0). The macOS kernel synthesized the container and MOUNTED BOTH VOLUMES: 'diskutil apfs list' showed 'Name: SAKVOLA ... Mount Point: /Volumes/SAKVOLA' and 'Name: SAKVOLB ... Mount Point: /Volumes/SAKVOLB'; 'diskutil unmountDisk force disk0' reported 'Forced unmount of all volumes on disk0 was successful'. Then Apple's NATIVE fsck_apfs (-n /dev/rdisk0) verified EACH volume and the container: '** Verifying volume /dev/rdisk1s1 ... The volume ... appears to be OK.', '** Verifying volume /dev/rdisk1s2 ... The volume SAKVOLB was formatted by S.A.K. Utility APFS writer and last modified by apfs_kext (2332.101.1) ... appears to be OK.', and '** Verifying allocated space. The container /dev/rdisk0 appears to be OK.' Evidence apfs.a4-kernel-mount-both-volumes.png + apfs.a4-fsck-both-volumes-PASS.png. The A4 lane flips to Yes.
